topic.name: Government Support for Local Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses government actions and policies aimed at supporting local communities through various initiatives, including property leasing for federal use, child protection measures, and agricultural grants.

Document 1: extends preferences in negotiating for the leasing of space for federal government use
given to private property owners and managers in jurisdictions in the National Capital region
Document 2: directs the Secretary of State to report annually to Congress regarding child protection compacts
countries with which the United States may enter into a child protection compact
Document 3: authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to make grants to municipalities
facilitate the ability of local farmers to grow food crops or raise livestock for local community sale

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Services
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to the Medicare program, specifically amendments that enhance access to services and payment structures for various healthcare providers.

Document 1: Assuring Medicare Stability and Access for Seniors Act of 2012
extend for one year the Medicare physician payment rates without change.
Document 2: Medicare Adult Day Services Act of 2015
cover certified adult day services furnished in a certified adult day services center and meeting specified requirements.
Document 3: Medicare Access to Rural Anesthesiology Act of 2013
provide payment under Medicare part A on a reasonable cost basis for anesthesia services furnished by a physician who is an anesthesiologist in certain rural hospitals.

topic.name: Education Funding and Teacher Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing financial support for students and teachers in the education system.

Document 1: requires the Department of Education to award an additional Pell Grant
total amount of the Pell Grants awarded may exceed the total maximum Pell Grant.
Document 2: amends the Elementary and Secondary Act of 1965
requires states and local educational agencies to use all of their funds under Title II of the Act to achieve compliance.
Document 3: expands the loan forgiveness available to public elementary and secondary school teachers
raises to $25,000 the amount of the FFEL or DL outstanding that may be forgiven after a public school teacher's fifth complete school year of teaching.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States focusing on temporary duty modifications for specific goods.

Document 1: reducing temporarily the duty on parts of machinery for the industrial preparation or manufacture of dried vegetable snack items.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain textile device covers and stands.
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on 2-Propenoic acid, polymer.

topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to health care and treatment for veterans.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to reimburse any non-VA medical provider...
Defines 'covered hospital care or medical services' as hospital care or medical services for which the veteran is eligible under the VA health care program.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to accept as sufficient proof of service-connection of post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)...
Requires the Secretary to conduct audits of examinations of veterans who submit claims for disability compensation for PTSD.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Development Policy
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at promoting and regulating oil and gas exploration and production in the United States, particularly in Alaska.

Document 1: the National Petroleum Reserve (NPR) in Alaska remains explicitly designated to provide oil and natural gas resources
require the mandatory program of competitive leasing of oil and gas in the NPR to include at least one lease sale annually
Document 2: Allows the governor of a state...to petition the Secretary of the Interior to make a new producing area of the state eligible for oil leasing
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit 45% of all rentals, royalties, bonus bids, and other sums due and payable to the United States from leases for new producing areas
Document 3: establish a competitive oil and gas leasing program on the Coastal Plain of Alaska
repeals the prohibition against production or leasing of oil and gas resources from the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge (ANWR)

topic.name: Immigration Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts related to immigration reform, focusing on the legal status and benefits for certain aliens in the United States.

Document 1: prohibits the use of funds to implement specified memoranda from the President
declared to have no statutory or constitutional basis
Document 2: amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional permanent resident status, an alien
Document 3: establish an employment-based, conditional immigrant visa
terminate the status of a sponsored entrepreneur if not meeting requirements

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on the oversight and reporting of foreign countries' electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Restoration
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, restoration, and designation of federal lands, focusing on forest health, wilderness preservation, and land exchanges.

Document 1: Amends the Healthy Forests Restoration Act of 2003 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) and the Secretary of the Interior...
Directs the Secretary to initiate salvage activities on federal land affected by a catastrophic event...
Document 2: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County...
Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to take necessary measures in such wilderness to control fire, insects, and diseases.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA), in any land exchange involving the conveyance of certain National Forest System (NFS) land...
Requires the deposit of any such payment into the fund established under the Sisk Act...

topic.name: Child Welfare and Privacy Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to child welfare services and the protection of individual privacy rights in the context of adoption, foster care, and geolocation data.

Document 1: prohibit federal payments for foster care and adoption assistance to a state or local government that discriminates against any entity
provides adoption or foster care services to only those couples who are united in marriage
Document 2: prohibit a covered entity from knowingly collecting or disclosing geolocation information
without the consent of the individual using the device
Document 3: allow foster care maintenance payments to be made on behalf of a child who is in a residential family treatment center
evaluate and report to Congress on the foster care maintenance payments

topic.name: Veteran and Mental Health Services
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving mental health and rehabilitation services for veterans and other populations.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the Office of National Drug Control Policy to delegate to the Department of Justice the authority to administer the veteran's treatment courts program
Programs that involve continuing judicial supervision over nonviolent offenders with substance abuse or mental health problems who have served in the U.S. military.
Document 2: Amends the Promoting Access to Medicare Act of 2014 to increase the number of states that may be selected to participate in Medicaid demonstration programs to improve community mental health services.
Document 3: Make permanent the special diabetes programs for Indians, which provide services for prevention and treatment of diabetes through the Indian Health Service.

topic.name: FDA Drug Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing the regulation, approval, and oversight of pharmaceuticals and controlled substances by the FDA.

Document 1: Directs the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA) to take actions to modify the approval of controlled-release oxycodone.
Limit any subsequent approval of any drug containing controlled-release oxycodone hychloride.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to apply debarment provisions to drug products.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to initiate debarment after a felony conviction.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act and the Public Health Service Act to delay the effective date of approval of a drug.
Establishes timelines for DOJ to register an applicant to manufacture a controlled substance.

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the restoration and management of water resources and ecosystems in various regions of the United States.

Document 1: Restoring the Everglades, an American Legacy Act of 2007
ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality
Document 2: South Orange County Recycled Water Enhancement Act
design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility and recycled water system
Document 3: San Gabriel Watershed Restoration Act
prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration, flood control, water quality control

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating and overseeing mortgage practices and the operations of government-sponsored enterprises in the housing finance system.

Document 1: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require that credit risk retention regulations ensure that there is no difference in the treatment of asset-backed securities securitized by the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) or the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac)
treatment of asset-backed securities securitized by any other entity.
Document 2: Amends the National Housing Act to remove the Director of the Enforcement Center from the Mortgagee Review Board of the Federal Housing Administration (FHA)
Sets forth mortgagee eligibility criteria.
Document 3: Requires the receipts and disbursements of the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to be counted as new budget authority
Subjects to the statutory public debt limit the face amount of obligations issued by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the conservation, management, and restoration of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to the Secretary of Commerce.
Authorizes funds for monitoring and assessment, research, pollution reduction, education, and technical support.
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities.
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds, in addition to projects addressing emerging priorities or threats.
Authorizes the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to undertake or authorize actions to minimize injury to a coral reef from vessel impacts.

topic.name: Agricultural and Nutritional Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to agricultural price support and nutritional programs aimed at enhancing food access for students.

Document 1: Suspends specified agricultural price support authorities under the Agricultural Adjustment Act of 1938
with regard to covered commodities, peanuts, sugar, and milk.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to implement a school-wide paperless free school meal program
to provide free meals to all their students without the use of paper applications.

Document 3: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to make foster children categorically eligible for free lunches and breakfasts.

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Climate Adaptation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and research initiatives aimed at promoting renewable energy solutions and adapting to climate change impacts, particularly in relation to energy efficiency and water management.

Document 1: 30% credit for qualified biomass fuel property expenditures
uses the burning of biomass fuel to heat a residence
Document 2: loans to electric utilities to reduce costs of complying with federal renewable energy standards
construction of renewable energy generation facilities
Document 3: directed and applied research to assist suppliers of drinking water in adapting to the effects of climate change
energy efficiency and greenhouse gas minimization

topic.name: Health Information Privacy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection and regulation of personally identifiable health information and the consequences of its unauthorized disclosure.

Document 1: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to expand requirements for reports to Congress on acquisition or disclosure of unsecured protected health information in a breach

Requires the Attorney General to report annually to Congress, and to publish, a study of complaints of alleged violations concerning wrongful disclosure of individually identifiable health information.
Document 2: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable protected health information
Makes any person who violates this Act liable to any person whose personally identifiable covered information has been disclosed.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to permit a video tape service provider to obtain a consumer's consent required to disclose personally identifiable information concerning the consumer
Requires consent to be distinct and separate from other legal or financial obligations.

topic.name: Tax Fraud and Identity Theft Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at combating tax fraud, identity theft, and related criminal activities involving taxpayer information.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the disclosure of tax return information in cases of identity theft
imposes new criminal and civil penalties for tax-related identity theft
Document 2: Prohibits officers or employees of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) from initiating any new audit of any taxpayer for 180 days
Document 3: requires U.S. entities to obtain and have an employer identification number (EIN) assigned by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS)
establishes civil penalties for the failure to have an EIN or provide required information

topic.name: Banking Regulation Reforms
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming and regulating banking institutions and their affiliations with securities firms.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (FDIA) to prohibit an insured depository institution from being an affiliate of any broker or dealer, investment adviser, investment company, or any other person or entity engaged principally in the issue, flotation, underwriting, public sale, or distribution of stocks, bonds, debentures, notes, or other securities.
Expands its prohibition against the transaction of banking activities by securities firms.
Document 2: This bill amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to exempt from specified titles of the Act 'safe and sound financial institutions.'

Exempts such institutions from the titles of the Act that address financial stability; orderly liquidation authority; improvements to regulation of bank and savings association holding companies and depository institutions; and mortgage reform and anti-predatory lending.
Document 3: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to instruct the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to take prompt corrective action to resolve the problems of a bank holding company.
Prohibits a bank holding company, except under certain conditions, from making a capital distribution if it would be undercapitalized after making the distribution.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at the conservation of endangered species and their habitats.

Document 1: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to require the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Commerce to make publicly available the best scientific and commercial data.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to submit an annual report detailing federal expenditures for civil actions brought under the ESA.
Document 2: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to apply the Act's prohibitions on importing or exporting species listed as threatened or endangered to species proposed to be listed as well.
The Government Accountability Office must conduct and submit a report on the results of a study of the effectiveness of trophy hunting in supporting international wildlife conservation efforts.
Document 3: Amends the Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp Act to allow the Department of the Interior to set the cost for Federal Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamps.
Limits the cost of the stamp to $25 for the first five years and $30 after that.

topic.name: Legislative Initiatives for Public Welfare
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures aimed at improving public welfare through infrastructure, education, and missing persons alerts.

Document 1: Vanceboro Dam Storage Project, West Branch Project, and Forest City Project, all located on the St. Croix River in Maine, are not required to be licensed by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.
provides that the Vanceboro Dam Storage Project... are not required to be licensed
Document 2: establishes a competitive grant program for states to develop or implement comprehensive plans to improve post-secondary outcomes for students.
must include reforms to: (1) implement measures to maintain or increase fiscal support for post-secondary education
Document 3: establish a national Silver Alert Communications Network within the Department of Justice (DOJ) to assist regional and local search efforts for missing seniors.
Directs the Attorney General to designate in DOJ a national Silver Alert Communications Network Coordinator.

topic.name: Rail Transportation Safety and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety and security within rail transportation systems, focusing on community impacts and regulatory oversight.

Document 1: Revises requirements with respect to the consolidation, merger, or acquisition of control involving rail carriers to require public hearings in the affected communities
Prohibits the Board from approving mergers if their adverse impacts on safety and on affected communities outweigh their transportation benefits.
Document 2: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to conduct a vulnerability assessment of freight and passenger rail transportation
Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to make grants to Amtrak for fire and life-safety improvements and infrastructure upgrades.

topic.name: Nuclear Energy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation, storage, and safety of nuclear energy and radioactive waste management.

Document 1: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to prohibit the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) from approving the renewal of a nuclear reactor license
renewal application was submitted more than 10 years before the license's expiration.
Document 2: Amends the Nuclear Waste Policy Act of 1982 to authorize the Department of Energy (DOE) to enter into new contracts
defines 'interim consolidated storage facility' as a facility licensed by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission for the storage of high-level radioactive waste or spent nuclear fuel

topic.name: Tax and Retirement Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative changes related to taxation and retirement benefits in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the marginal income tax rates with a single rate of 17% on individual taxable income.
Revises rules relating to transfers of excess pension assets.
Document 2: This bill amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) and the Internal Revenue Code to authorize the electronic delivery of pension plan documents.
An electronically furnished document must be prepared and furnished in a manner that is consistent with the style, format, and content requirements for the document.
Document 3: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA), the Internal Revenue Code, and the Public Health Service Act to extend the continuation of group health care benefits.
Extends benefits to certain recipients of benefits paid by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation (PBGC).

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and resources for small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to allow the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to employ additional export finance specialists
Requires the Office to encourage the participation of SBA employees and resource partners in reverse trade missions hosted or sponsored by the federal government.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to require the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to develop a program to award cash prizes
Requires a prize recipient to provide assistance to at least 10 new small businesses per year.
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to increase the amount that a state or local governmental entity may invest in a small business investment company
Requires the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to approve within 60 days after receipt an application for licensing as an SBIC.

topic.name: Alaska Native Rights and Programs
topic.description: Legislation focused on the rights, land entitlements, and health programs for Alaska Native communities.

Document 1: Authorizes Sealaska Corporation to select and receive conveyance of its remaining land entitlement under the ANCSA from federal land in southeast Alaska.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to complete the conveyance of lands to Sealaska within one year of selection.
Document 2: Authorize the Secretary to award grants to states and Indian tribes for comprehensive osteoporosis and related bone disease control and prevention programs.
Document 3: Amends the Indian Employment, Training and Related Services Demonstration Act of 1992 to make revisions to the program that provides for the integration of employment, training, and related services programs from federal funds.

topic.name: Social Security Act Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Social Security Act focusing on disability benefits and related programs.

Document 1: eliminate the waiting period for disability insurance benefits for terminally ill individuals.
Document 2: prohibit the Commissioner of Social Security from considering any medical evidence with respect to whether an individual is under a disability which is furnished by individuals convicted of Medicare fraud.

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the federal agency rulemaking process and enhancing transparency in economic regulations.

Document 1: revises the federal agency rulemaking process
require agencies to promulgate only rules that are required by law
Document 2: requires each federal agency to submit a monthly report to the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs
OIRA must make such monthly reports publicly available on the Internet
Document 3: directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to conduct an empirical study
requires the study to be made available to the public for notice and comment

topic.name: Iran Sanctions and Nuclear Disarmament
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to sanctions against Iran and efforts towards nuclear disarmament and arms control.

Document 1: Requires the government to negotiate and enter into a multilateral treaty for the dismantlement and elimination of all nuclear weapons.
Promote policies to induce all other countries to join in such commitments.
Document 2: Prohibits the Department of the Treasury from issuing a license for transactions that would benefit the government of Iran.
Document 3: Prescribes requirements for sanctions with respect to Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps and related entities.
The bill expresses the sense of Congress with respect to human rights in Iran.

topic.name: Housing Assistance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving housing assistance and development programs for low-income and vulnerable populations in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937
exempt a small public housing agency from the requirement to prepare an annual public agency plan
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to provide financial assistance
develop the capacity of community-based housing development organizations
Document 3: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937
exclude as family income for housing assistance purposes any Department of Veterans Affairs payments

topic.name: Campaign Finance Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating campaign finance practices and lobbying activities related to federal elections.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit an authorized committee of a candidate for election to the U.S. Senate or the House of Representatives from making any expenditure of funds in support of the candidate's election campaign that were not deposited in the committee's account during the election cycle for the office involved.
Makes an exception to this prohibition for candidates facing certain opponents who make expenditures from personal funds exceeding specified dollar amounts.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit any authorized committee of a candidate or any other political committee established, maintained, or controlled by a candidate or person who holds a federal office from employing the spouse or any immediate family member of such candidate or federal office holder.

Amends the federal criminal code to subject to specified criminal and civil penalties any spouse of a Member of Congress who: (1) was not a registered lobbyist at least one year before the Member's election to Congress; and (2) knowingly lobbies, after the election of such Member, any Member on behalf of a client for compensation or is associated with any such lobbying activity by his or her employer.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit any authorized committee of a candidate or any other political committee established, maintained, or controlled by a candidate or person who holds a federal office from employing the spouse or any immediate family member of such candidate or federal office holder.

Amends the federal criminal code to subject to specified criminal and civil penalties any spouse of a Member of Congress who: (1) was not a registered lobbyist at least one year before the Member's election to Congress; but (2) knowingly lobbies any Member of Congress on behalf of a client for compensation; or (3) is associated with any such lobbying activity by his or her employer.

topic.name: Consumer Credit and Reporting Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving consumer access to credit information and protecting consumer rights in credit reporting.

Document 1: amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act
authorize a person or the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to furnish to a consumer reporting agency information relating to the performance of a consumer
Document 2: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to require nationwide consumer reporting agencies to provide a free annual credit score disclosure
including nationwide specialty consumer reporting agencies

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating telecommunications services, including caller ID accuracy and digital television transition efforts.

Document 1: Caller ID Scam Prevention Act of 2014
prohibition on the provision of inaccurate caller identification information
Document 2: Short-term Analog Flash and Emergency Readiness Act
continued broadcasting in the analog television service
Document 3: Digital TV Transition Fairness Act
coupons that can be applied toward the cost to install a digital-to-analog converter box

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at providing benefits and support to military veterans and their families.

Document 1: waive the monthly part B premium with respect to military retired or retainer pay
the spouse, widow, or widower of such individuals
Document 2: extends the termination of Servicemembers' Group Life Insurance coverage for totally disabled veterans
entitles a surviving spouse to a benefit for the month of a veteran's death
Document 3: makes National Guard and Selected Reserve members deployed to augment active forces eligible for Post-9/11 educational assistance
transitional post-demobilization health benefits for a member who is separated from active duty

topic.name: Environmental Health Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating products and practices to protect public health and the environment.

Document 1: Requires a cleaning product to bear a label listing each of its ingredients
Increases penalties for violations of FHSA.
Document 2: Directs the Department of the Army to act as the lead agency with respect to radioactive contamination remediation
Nothing in this Act affects the site's designation as a Superfund site.
Document 3: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to convene a working group
Establishes pilot programs to explore new technologies.

topic.name: Labor and Employment Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving labor conditions, skills development, and employee rights within the workforce.

Document 1: establishes an Office of Apprenticeship
promote stronger connections between the registered apprenticeship programs involved and participating two- and four-year postsecondary educational institutions
Document 2: Repeals specified exceptions to rights of certain Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) employees to engage in collective bargaining
subjects such decision to judicial review in the appropriate U.S. District Court
Document 3: Grants an employee the right to request that his or her employer change the terms and conditions of employment
requires the employer to engage in a timely, good faith interactive process with the employee

topic.name: Counterterrorism Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislative measures aimed at preventing terrorism, managing detainees, and curbing financial resources for terrorist organizations.

Document 1: prohibits U.S. agency funds from being used to transfer an individual detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay

expresses the sense of Congress that any individual who was detained at Guantanamo and is suspected of or confirmed by U.S. intelligence as having re-engaged in terrorist activity should be arrested and returned to detention at Guantanamo.
Document 2: prohibits the Department of Justice from prosecuting any unprivileged enemy combatant for any offense.
Document 3: expresses the sense of Congress regarding the importance of the United States and its partners curtailing the financial resources of the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL)
The President may designate a country as a Jurisdiction of Terrorism Financing Concern.

topic.name: Education and Public Health Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to public health requirements in schools and educational policies affecting local educational agencies.

Document 1: prohibit the Department of Health and Human Services from awarding grants to public entities of a state for preventive health service programs unless the state requires each student in public elementary or secondary school to be vaccinated
provides an exception for students whose health would be endangered by vaccination
Document 2: compensate local educational agencies [LEAs] for the financial burden of federal activities affecting their areas
educating children who are admitted into this country as citizens of one of the Freely Associated States
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to revise the process for blocking the access of military recruiters
Requires ESEA-assisted local educational agencies (LEAs) to notify each secondary school student's parent of the option to submit a written request to the LEA

topic.name: Voting Rights for Military Personnel
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring voting access and registration for military members and overseas citizens during federal elections.

Document 1: requires each state to ensure that absentee ballots are sent to absent uniformed services voters and overseas voters
not later than 45 days before the state's deadline for receiving absentee ballots
Document 2: require designating direct each Secretary of a military department to designate an office on each Armed Forces installation
to ensure that certain individuals are provided the opportunity to register to vote in federal elections

Document 3: Divides the United States into six regions of specified states to provide for interregional primary elections and caucuses

topic.name: Legislation on Animal and Firearm Protection
topic.description: This cluster focuses on federal legislation aimed at preventing cruelty to animals and regulating firearms and ammunition to enhance public safety.

Document 1: Prevention of Equine Cruelty Act of 2008
impose a fine and/or prison term for possessing, shipping, transporting, purchasing, selling, delivering, or receiving any horse with the intent that it be slaughtered for human consumption.
Document 2: Anti-Large Capacity Ammunition Feeding Device Act of 2007
Reinstates criminal provisions prohibiting the possession or transfer of large capacity ammunition feeding devices.
Document 3: Stop Gun Trafficking and Strengthen Law Enforcement Act of 2011
prohibit any person from receiving, or transferring or otherwise disposing of two or more firearms that have been shipped or transported in interstate or foreign commerce.

topic.name: Federal Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety standards and environmental protections in various sectors including aviation and energy.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate standards for mercury emissions from electric utility steam generating units.
Document 2: Amends federal transportation law to authorize the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (Transportation Security Administration) to designate certain airport areas as Federal Special Security Zones.
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to develop and submit to Congress a strategic runway safety plan.

topic.name: Food Safety and Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on ensuring food safety standards and regulating the claims made about food and dietary supplements.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to provide that a food or dietary supplement is not a drug solely because a claim is made regarding legitimate scientific research
Prohibits the Secretary of Health and Human Services from restricting the distribution of truthful and not misleading information on legitimate scientific research.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act, the Poultry Products Inspection Act, and the Egg Products Inspection Act to include in the definition of 'adulterated' a product bearing or containing a pathogen
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to identify significant foodborne disease pathogens and establish public health goals to reduce foodborne illness.
Document 3: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to prohibit schools and service institutions from serving food that contains trans fats
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a process for granting hardship waivers to schools or service institutions.

topic.name: Support for Minority Institutions
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing the rights, resources, and educational opportunities for minority-serving institutions and communities in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes within the National Science Foundation (NSF) an Office of Minority Serving Institution Digital and Wireless Technology
Makes the following institutions eligible for such assistance: a historically Black college or university; a Hispanic-, Alaska Native-, or Native Hawaiian-serving institution.
Document 2: Establishes the U.S. Office for Native Hawaiian Affairs within the Office of the Secretary of the Interior
Recognizes the right of the Native Hawaiian people to adopt organic governing documents.
Document 3: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages of Haines, Ketchikan, Petersburg, Tenakee, and Wrangell, Alaska, to organize as Urban Corporations
Authorize those Urban Corporations to establish a settlement trust to promote the health, education, and welfare of the trust beneficiaries.

topic.name: Veterans' Legal Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legal protections and rights for veterans, particularly regarding employment and civil relief following military service.

Document 1: Amends the sanctions provisions in Rule 11 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
Requires any sanction to compensate parties injured by the conduct in question.
Document 2: Expands the definition of 'service in the uniformed services' for the purposes of uniformed servicemembers' employment and reemployment rights

Requires a person submitting an application for reemployment due to such an absence for medical treatment to provide the employer, upon request, with documentation to establish eligibility for reemployment.
Document 3: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act of 1940 to extend from 90 days to one year the period following release from active duty
during which a member of the Armed Forces shall be protected from mortgage foreclosure.

topic.name: Expansion of USPS Services
topic.description: Legislation aimed at expanding the services and powers of the U.S. Postal Service to include nonpostal and financial services.

1: Expands the enumerated powers of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to include the authority to provide nonpostal services.
Removes existing conditions that limit nonpostal services to certain services offered as of January 1, 2006.
2: Directs the U.S. Postal Service to issue a semipostal to benefit the Peace Corps.
3: Expands the specific powers of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to include the provision of basic financial services.
Includes small-dollar loans, checking accounts, and other basic financial services.

topic.name: Community Policing and Crime Prevention
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing community policing, law enforcement training, and crime prevention initiatives through grant funding.

Document 1: expanding the authority of the Attorney General to make competitive grants under the public safety and community policing grant program
establish and implement innovative programs to reduce and prevent illegal drug activities
Document 2: requires a state to return any unobligated Community Oriented Policing Services Program (COPS) funds
state ineligible to receive any COPS funding until it has in effect laws and policies that prohibit the issuance of a license to such an alien
Document 3: allow the use of public safety and community policing grants to hire and train new career law enforcement officers
authorize the Attorney General to give preference to grant applications for the hiring and training of new career law enforcement officers

topic.name: Reproductive Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures related to parental consent for sexual education, abortion funding restrictions, and reproductive health care access.

Document 1: Requires each preschool and elementary and secondary school that receives federal funds to obtain the affirmative, informed, written consent of a child's parent before requesting information from, or conveying information to, such child on topics relating to sex or sexuality.
Subjects individuals and schools to civil liability and fines for violating these consent and disclosure requirements.
Document 2: Amends the prohibition on using funds available to the Department of Defense (DOD) to perform abortions by adding an exception for cases where the pregnancy is the result of rape or incest.
Repeals a statutory restriction on using a medical treatment facility or other DOD facility to perform an abortion.
Document 3: Prohibits the expenditure of funds authorized or appropriated by federal law for any abortion.
Creates a cause of action for any violations.

topic.name: Emergency Preparedness and Health
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing emergency response capabilities and health-related initiatives to support individuals affected by disasters and health conditions like asthma.

Document 1: establish at least six national emergency centers on military installations
temporary housing, medical, and humanitarian assistance to individuals and families dislocated due to an emergency
Document 2: authorize additional appropriations to expand the National Asthma Education and Prevention Program
collaborate with states to expand the scope of asthma-related activities
Document 3: award matching grants to local educational agencies to purchase automated external defibrillators
provide training to meet the grant requirement that at least five adult employees or volunteers at each school successfully complete training in its use

topic.name: Victim Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing support and restitution for victims of crimes, particularly in cases of sexual abuse and terrorism.

Document 1: mandatory restitution to require a court to apply the principle of aggregate causation
jointly and severally liable for the victim's losses
Document 2: allow a borrower who is a victim of a terrorist attack to defer student loans
Document 3: designate an independent advocate for campus sexual assault prevention and response
represent the interests of each student victim

topic.name: District of Columbia Legislative Changes
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts that amend the governance and judicial structure of the District of Columbia.

Document 1: Amends the District of Columbia Home Rule Act
Revises the D.C. Council authority to increase spending
Document 2: Establish the Office of the District Attorney for the District of Columbia
Requires the DA to be elected to a four-year term
Document 3: Amends the federal judicial code
Modifies the citizenship rules to treat corporations as citizens

topic.name: Regulation of Smuggling Activities
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at preventing and regulating various forms of smuggling, including cash and goods, particularly in relation to international trade and transportation.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to require reports concerning means of access to funds
Revises penalties and procedures regarding smuggling of cash, monetary instruments
Document 2: Prohibits the head of each executive agency from awarding a contract for the procurement of Chinese goods
Limits the total value of Chinese goods that may be procured by the U.S. government
Document 3: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 with respect to aviation smuggling
Extend its coverage of aircraft to ultralight vehicles

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Assistance Policies
topic.description: The documents discuss various U.S. legislative acts aimed at improving global health, education, and development in foreign countries.

Document 1: Directs the President to call the White House Conference on Haiti to assist in Haiti's recovery and long-term development.
Requires congressional committee hearings on the recommendations, status reports, and recovery and long-term development of Haiti.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to establish a program to develop technologies designed to improve health and nutrition in developing countries.
Directs USAID to report annually to Congress on USAID research and development activities.
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to state that it is U.S. policy to work with foreign countries to achieve universal basic education.
Authorizes the President to provide funds and assistance to develop national basic education plans.

topic.name: Workplace Benefits and Financial Literacy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving workplace benefits for employees and enhancing financial literacy among students and workers.

Document 1: credit against payroll tax liability for a payroll increase
employers that employ fewer than 100 employees
Document 2: provide annually to each of their covered full-time employees at least seven days of paid sick time
prohibited acts by an employer, and penalties for violations
Document 3: require institutions of higher education to provide student borrowers with financial literacy counseling
financial literacy counseling to include information on banking, budgeting and saving, credit and debt management

topic.name: Tax-Exempt Bond Financing
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the issuance of tax-exempt bonds for financing specific projects, including water supply technologies and agricultural processing.

Document 1: tax-exempt clean renewable water supply bonds
finance certain water supply technologies
Document 2: tax-exempt small issue bonds
finance the purchase of property or land used primarily for the processing of agricultural products
Document 3: tax-exempt bond financing
fixed-wing aircraft used to provide acute care emergency medical services

topic.name: LGBTQ+ Rights Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting and affirming the rights of LGBTQ+ individuals in various sectors, including marriage, employment, and healthcare.

Document 1: Amends the Defense of Marriage Act to repeal provisions allowing states to give no effect to a public act respecting a relationship between persons of the same sex that is treated as a marriage.
An individual shall be considered married if that individual's marriage is valid in the state where the marriage was entered into.
Document 2: Prohibits any federal employee from discriminating on the basis of sexual orientation.
Affirms that discrimination against federal employees on the basis of sexual orientation is already prohibited under current law.
Document 3: Prohibits discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation against any person in the United States under any health care service.
Prohibits discrimination against any person in the United States under any health care service or research program.

topic.name: Government Commissions and Acts
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts that establish commissions aimed at addressing specific governmental functions and societal issues.

Document 1: establishes the John F. Kennedy Centennial Commission
provide advice and assistance to federal, state, and local governmental agencies
Document 2: Establishes in the legislative branch the Commission on Long Term Social Security Solvency
Requires expedited consideration of any proposed legislation approving Commission recommendations
Document 3: Establishes in the executive branch a Commission on Evidence-Based Policymaking
make recommendations on how data infrastructure and protocols should be modified

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public health through the regulation of food and beverage safety, prevention of youth violence, and taxation of unhealthy products.

Document 1: impose an excise tax on the sale or transfer of any specified sugar-sweetened beverage product
funding programs and research to reduce the human and economic costs of diabetes, obesity, dental caries, and other diet-related health conditions
Document 2: promote safety and combat youth violence and substance abuse in public middle or secondary schools
reduce violence and drug, alcohol, and tobacco addiction among students
Document 3: limit the quantity of total arsenic and lead in beverages containing fruit juice
a beverage containing fruit juice shall be deemed to be adulterated if the quantity of total arsenic or lead exceeds the limits

topic.name: Sanctions and Human Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislation aimed at enforcing sanctions and promoting human rights in specific countries, including Belarus, North Korea, and Burma.

Document 1: U.S.-imposed sanctions with respect to Belarus remain in effect
the government of Belarus has met specified conditions under the Belarus Democracy Act of 2004
Document 2: authorize the President to investigate credible information of sanctionable activities involving North Korea
engaged in significant financial transactions, relating to the manufacture, maintenance, or use of any such weapon or system
Document 3: Prohibits FY2014 or FY2015 funds from being made available for security assistance to the government of Burma unless the Secretary of State certifies to Congress
Burma has taken steps toward establishing civilian oversight of the armed forces, addressing human rights abuses by the military

topic.name: Autoimmune and Infectious Disease Research
topic.description: Legislative initiatives focused on research, prevention, and awareness of autoimmune and tick-borne diseases.

Document 1: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for research on environmental triggers that can cause the initiation of autoimmune diseases
conduct public and professional awareness activities regarding autoimmune diseases
Document 2: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to conduct a comprehensive study to compare total health outcomes between vaccinated and unvaccinated U.S. populations
Document 3: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish the Tick-Borne Diseases Advisory Committee
ensure interagency coordination and communication regarding efforts to address tick-borne diseases

topic.name: Legislation on Technology and Security
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to technology, security, and consumer protection in the digital realm.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require commercial mobile service providers to make available on mobile devices a function that an account holder may use remotely to delete or render inaccessible all information relating to the account holder.

Prohibits a mobile device from being manufactured in the United States or imported into the United States for sale or resale to the public, unless the device is configured in such a manner that a service provider may make such remote deletion and inoperability functions available on the device.
Document 2: This bill amends the federal criminal code to establish a certification process with respect to nondisclosure requirements imposed in connection with specified administrative subpoenas.
a judicial review process with respect to the imposition of such requirements.
Document 3: Authorizes each member state under the Streamlined Sales and Use Tax Agreement to require all sellers not qualifying for the small-seller exception to collect and remit sales and use taxes for remote sales.
Provides that such taxing authority shall commence no sooner than six months after such state enacts legislation to specify the tax or taxes to which the simplification requirements apply.

topic.name: Occupational Safety and Health Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving safety and health standards across various industries, including mining and pipeline operations.

Document 1: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act to revise requirements for the Secretary of Labor's continuing evaluation of approved state occupational safety and health plans.
Requires the Secretary to determine whether a state that fails to comply substantially with the provisions of a plan should be given the opportunity to remedy such deficiencies.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to establish an independent National Mine Safety Board.
Requires mine inspections to be conducted during various shifts and days of the week when miners are normally present.
Document 3: Requires each operator of a gas pipeline facility, in accordance with an integrity management program, to accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping or equipment that is leaking.

Directs the Administrator of the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration to issue non-binding best practices guidelines for identifying and classifying high-risk pipeline infrastructure and leaks for repair or replacement.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at amending existing laws and enhancing oversight mechanisms within various government sectors.

Document 1: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2014
Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation payments.
Document 2: Judicial Transparency and Ethics Enhancement Act of 2013
Establishes the Office of Inspector General for the Judicial Branch.

topic.name: Sports Regulation and Gambling
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation of sports, gambling practices, and the safety of professional athletes in combat sports.

Document 1: Amends the federal judicial code to exempt a lottery, sweepstakes, or other betting, gambling, or waging scheme operating exclusively in New Jersey.
from the prohibition against a governmental entity, or a person acting pursuant to the law or compact of a governmental entity, sponsoring, operating, advertising, or promoting sports gambling.
Document 2: Amends the Sports Broadcasting Act of 1961 to deny the antitrust exemption for joint agreements covering the telecasting of sports contests.
subject the conduct, acts, practices, or agreements of persons in the business of organized professional major league baseball to the antitrust laws.
Document 3: This bill amends the Professional Boxing Safety Act of 1996 to establish definitions for 'fighter,' 'combat sport competition,' and 'mixed martial arts'.
requires the Association of Boxing Commissions to establish guidelines for minimum contractual provisions that should be included in bout agreements.

topic.name: U.S. Military and Foreign Relations
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislation related to U.S. military personnel, security services, and foreign assistance in conflict zones and partnerships.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of State to ensure that only government personnel provide security services at U.S. diplomatic or consular missions in Iraq or Afghanistan.
Requires the President to report to Congress on the status of planning for the use of government and military personnel instead of private contractors.
Document 2: Specifies principles that Congress declares should be the basis of the relationship between the United States and Pakistan.
Authorizes the President to provide assistance to Pakistan to support democratic institutions and combat militant extremism.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that the federal government should support private sector employment initiatives for military personnel.
Authorizes the Chief of the National Guard Bureau to award grants to nonprofit organizations to assist military personnel in securing employment.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Actions on Foreign Relations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at regulating foreign relations, particularly concerning terrorism, energy resources, and media broadcasting.

Document 1: Directs the President to continue any federal investigative activities regarding the bombing of Pan Am flight 103 and any other terrorist attacks against U.S. citizens attributable to the government of Muammar Qaddafi.
Prohibits the President from distributing property confiscated from Muammar Qaddafi until the President certifies to Congress that the TNC or successor government is cooperating.
Document 2: Permits U.S. persons to engage in any transaction necessary for exploration and extraction of hydrocarbon resources from any portion of a foreign exclusive economic zone.
Amends the Trade Sanctions Reform and Export Enhancement Act of 2000 to instruct the Secretary of the Treasury to authorize certain travel-related transactions for travel to Cuba.

topic.name: National Security and Crime Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing national security and addressing serious crimes, particularly those involving terrorism and child exploitation.

Document 1: No Fly for Foreign Fighters Act
threat to aviation or national security
Document 2: Prevention and Deterrence of Crimes Against Children Act
increase criminal penalties for offenses relating to the sexual exploitation of children

topic.name: Coal and Water Resource Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management and regulation of coal mining and water resources in the context of environmental and health considerations.

Document 1: Prohibits the use of funds made available to the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to implement policies regarding coal mining operations.
Requires notice and opportunity for comment in accordance with the Administrative Procedure Act.
Document 2: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 to revise the formula for determining transfers from the Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund.
Considers beneficiaries eligible to receive health benefits under the Plan.
Document 3: Amends the Colorado River Storage Project Act to authorize the construction of the Northwestern New Mexico Rural Water Supply Project.
Directs the Secretary to identify projects recommended to repair, rehabilitate, or reconstruct water delivery systems.

topic.name: Health and Compensation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on health care access and compensation for specific health-related claims.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1993 regarding waivers of small miner claim maintenance fees
qualifying for specified relief under the Act, including opportunity to cure.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and nonprofit private health care providers
Requires the Secretary to ensure that such grants cover a variety of diabetes-related health care services.
Document 3: Amends the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act to extend the Radiation Exposure Compensation Trust Fund
Increases the amount of compensation an individual filing a claim may receive to $150,000.

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Emergency Services
topic.description: This cluster discusses tax-related legislation aimed at supporting and incentivizing volunteer emergency services and financial accountability in government programs.

Document 1: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008
Requires payment into the Treasury for reduction of the public debt
Document 2: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions allowing a tax exclusion for benefits paid to volunteer firefighters
increasing the amount of benefits excludible
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow sponsors of certain deferred compensation plans
exempting a length of service award program from treatment as an employee pension benefit plan

topic.name: Judicial Oversight and Regulations
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of judicial oversight and the regulatory frameworks governing specific legal processes in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes a pilot program in certain U.S. district courts
designated by the chief judge to hear them
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978
authorize the appointment of additional judges to the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court
Document 3: Prohibits federal funds from being used by a federal agency
to participate in a lawsuit to overturn, enjoin, or otherwise invalidate specified immigration-related laws

topic.name: Tax Incentives and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing tax relief and regulatory frameworks to support small businesses, healthcare professionals, and transportation services.

Document 1: increase the tax deduction for business start-up expenditures
reduce from 35% to 15% the alternative tax on the net capital gains of C corporations
Document 2: require certain fuel costs disclosures and fuel costs reimbursements
Prohibits any person from causing a motor carrier, broker, or freight forwarder to present false or misleading information
Document 3: allow a physician a tax deduction equal to the amount such physician would have otherwise charged for charity medical care
Imposes an overall limitation on such deduction equal to 10% of the physician's gross income

topic.name: Veterans and Family Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at supporting veterans and recognizing family partnerships within the context of immigration and benefits.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include a 'permanent partner'
Defines 'permanent partnership' as the relationship existing between two permanent partners.
Document 2: Amends the Veterans' Benefits Programs Improvement Act of 1991
Authorize the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to sell for fair market value Pershing Hall.
Document 3: Extends eligibility for hospital care, medical services, or nursing home care for veterans.
Enrollment must be within three years of such enactment.

topic.name: Climate and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislation related to climate, weather, and environmental management, including NOAA's establishment, livestock disaster assistance, and air quality regulations.

Document 1: Reestablishes in the Department of Commerce the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA)
programs to support efforts, on a continuing basis, to collect data and provide information and products regarding satellites, observations, and coastal, ocean, and Great Lakes information
Document 2: Makes livestock indemnity payments through FY2013 to eligible producers on farms that have incurred excess livestock death losses due to adverse weather
Provides assistance for forage losses due to drought on land that is native or improved pasture land
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to exclude from the definition of the term 'air pollutant' carbon dioxide, water vapor, methane, nitrous oxide
declare that nothing in the Act shall be treated as authorizing or requiring the regulation of climate change or global warming

topic.name: Government Employment and Health Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to employment suitability criteria for government positions and health initiatives aimed at improving children's health.

Document 1: establish criteria to determine whether an individual is suitable for employment with the District of Columbia government
Prohibits a hiring authority from making a new determination under this Act for an individual who has already been determined suitable or fit based on character or conduct
Document 2: Redesignates the Ocmulgee National Monument in Georgia as the Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park
conduct a special resource study of the Ocmulgee River corridor
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to make five grants to local educational agencies
Requires screening programs, subject to parental consent, to screen children every six months during the school year

topic.name: Legislation for Community Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at promoting economic development, environmental protection, and community welfare in specific regions of the United States.

Document 1: Monroe County, Pennsylvania, shall be a HUBZone
for purposes of the Small Business Act
Document 2: Establishes the Jupiter Inlet Lighthouse Outstanding Natural Area
to protect, conserve, and enhance the unique and nationally important historic, natural, cultural, scientific, educational, scenic, and recreational values
Document 3: increases the number of acres on which agricultural producers are authorized to grow fruits and vegetables
in a food desert, which is a census tract that has a poverty rate of at least 20%

topic.name: Financial Institution Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at regulating financial institutions and improving access to banking services for underserved consumers.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to prohibit an insured credit union from making any member business loan that would result in the total amount of such loans outstanding at that credit union at any one time exceeding either: (1) 1.75 times the actual net worth of the credit union, or (2) 12.25% of the total assets of the credit union.

Directs the Board to develop a tiered approval process, including lending standards, under which an insured credit union gradually increases the amount of member business lending in a manner that is consistent with safe and sound operations.
Document 2: Authorizes an appropriate federal or state banking agency to charter and license a de novo bridging bank depository institution to enable underbanked and unbanked consumers to transition or return to the financial mainstream.

Defines underbanked consumers as individuals or families who have a deposit account with an insured depository institution or credit union, but who have limited or no ability to access nondepository services from such institutions.
Document 3: This bill amends the Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council Act of 1978 to require a federal financial institutions regulatory agency to make a final examination report to a financial institution within 60 days after the later of: (1) the exit interview for an examination of the institution, or (2) the provision of additional information by the institution relating to the examination.
Financial institutions may appeal a material supervisory determination contained in a final report of examination.

topic.name: Tax and Veterans Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to taxation and benefits for veterans and non-profit organizations.

Document 1: Disabled Veterans Insurance Act of 2007
maximum amount of supplemental service disabled veterans' insurance for totally disabled veterans.
Document 2: Sunlight for Unaccountable Non-profits (SUN) Act
require the annual tax return information for tax-exempt organizations to be made available to the public.
Document 3: Close Foreign Tax Loopholes: Make it in America Act of 2010
Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the taxation of foreign income.

topic.name: Veterans and Tax Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to veterans' benefits and tax credits.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to include children of Vietnam War and certain Korean War veterans
meeting the definition of minimum essential coverage under requirements of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
Document 2: Prohibits the use of any funds appropriated for FY2014 for flag or general officers
excess to the number of such officers serving in such military department
Document 3: First-Time Homebuyer Tax Credit Guarantee Act of 2009
extend the first-time homebuyer tax credit for a 90-day period after its termination date

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing U.S. national security through various measures related to disaster response, terrorism compensation, and intelligence sharing.

Document 1: Establishes the United States Commission on Planetary Defense to review response efforts to dangerous Near-Earth Objects (NEOs)
assess U.S. and foreign technology readiness levels required to provide effective planetary defense.
Document 2: Amends the September 11th Victim Compensation Fund of 2001 to expand eligibility for compensation
establishes the United States Victims of State Sponsored Terrorism Fund to compensate victims of state-sponsored terrorism.
Document 3: Allows financial assistance for terrorism prevention activities to be used to pay the salaries of intelligence analysts
facilitate information and intelligence sharing activities.

topic.name: Environmental Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating environmental impacts of oil and gas exploration and protecting vulnerable populations from hazardous substances.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to direct the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to publish in the Federal Register
Requires the Secretary to consult with the Secretaries of Commerce or of the Interior regarding any endangered species or fisheries
Document 2: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations requiring
Requires consideration of the need for different technology requirements in different environments and depths
Document 3: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to publish a health advisory for hexavalent chromium
Establish a national primary drinking water regulation for hexavalent chromium that is protective of vulnerable individuals

topic.name: Environmental and Health Regulations
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative acts related to environmental protection, land management, and health regulations concerning biological materials.

Document 1: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to determine the quantity of each greenhouse gas
Directs each covered entity to report to the Administrator its greenhouse gas emissions for the prior calendar year.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to acquire by donation or by purchase
Requires any lands acquired under this Act to be administered by the Secretary as part of Homestead National Monument.
Document 3: Amends the National Organ Transplant Act to prohibit acquiring, receiving, or otherwise transferring a cord blood unit
if the transfer affects interstate commerce.

topic.name: Regulatory Reforms on Business Practices
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at regulating business practices related to labor relations, consumer communications, and drug testing integrity.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to repeal the authority of the General Counsel of the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to issue, and prosecute before the Board, complaints with respect to unfair labor practices.
Limits the Board's rulemaking authority to rules concerning the internal functions of the Board.
Document 2: Requires business entities that initiate or receive certain customer service communications to disclose their physical location if located outside the United States.
Treats such a business entity's failure to comply as a regulatory violation under the Federal Trade Commission Act (FTCA) regarding unfair or deceptive acts or practices.
Document 3: Makes it unlawful to knowingly manufacture, market, sell, ship, or otherwise provide to another individual any product with the intent to assist such individual to use such product to defraud a drug test.
Treats a violation of this Act as an unfair or deceptive act or practice under the Federal Trade Commission Act.

topic.name: Consumer Protection and Transparency
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer safety and transparency in various sectors including child safety, education, and manufacturing.

Document 1: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promulgate regulations requiring warning labels on baby monitors to alert users that video and audio from such devices may be intercepted and viewed or heard by potential intruders outside their home.
Document 2: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require each coeducational institution of higher education (IHE) that is participating in a title IV program and has an intercollegiate athletic program to include in its annual report on its intercollegiate athletic program: the amount of revenue generated by each item on a list of categorized sources, disaggregated by each sports team.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a voluntary America Star Program under which manufacturers may have products certified as meeting the standards of labels that indicate to consumers the extent to which the products are manufactured in the United States.

topic.name: Community Support Programs
topic.description: Legislative initiatives aimed at enhancing community support, welfare, and reintegration for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: awarding competitive, matching Promise Neighborhood Partnership grants to nonprofit organizations
improving the academic achievement, health and social development, and college and career readiness of children
Document 2: humane treatment, handling, and disposition of nonambulatory livestock
humanely euthanize nonambulatory livestock
Document 3: establish a national combat veteran reintegration program
provide National Guard members and their families with information, services, referral, and outreach opportunities

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Safety and Health
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at enhancing safety for workers and improving health insurance coverage.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to make grants to one not-for-profit organization to develop a roadway safety training institute
to deliver comprehensive and uniform roadway safety training to roadway workers across the United States.
Document 3: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA), the Public Health Service Act, and the Internal Revenue Code to prohibit a group health plan from imposing any preexisting condition exclusion
Requires each health insurance issuer offering coverage in the group market in a state to accept every employer in the state that applies for such coverage.

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various federal legislative acts that regulate specific industries and land use in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to grant the owner of a human generic drug facility a waiver
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to Solano County
all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to the parcel of approximately 82 acres of land
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to grant a right of way for natural gas pipelines
Defines 'gas gathering line and associated field compression unit' as: (1) a pipeline installed to transport natural gas production

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts passed by the U.S. Congress addressing security, health, and environmental issues.

Document 1: Iraq Security Agreement Act of 2008
Directs the President to report annually to the appropriate congressional committees respecting U.S. security commitments
Document 2: Lifelong Improvements in Food and Exercise (LIFE) Act

Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to carry out a national program to conduct and support activities regarding individuals who are overweight or obese
Document 3: Efficient Marine Mammal Protection Act
This bill abolishes the Marine Mammal Commission and transfers to the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service its functions

topic.name: Public Policy and Funding
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislation related to funding and support for public services, including transportation and veterans' healthcare.
