Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to make emergency grants to states
Requires amounts apportioned to a state or urbanized area to be used for operating expenses necessary to restore or prevent a reduction in public transportation service.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Power Act to repeal provisions regarding mandatory fair market value compensation for right-of-way acquisitions
Provides that the permit holder may acquire the right-of-way in accordance with state law.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to establish a two-year pilot program to provide covered veterans with non-invasive techniques
Includes as a covered veteran one who has a service-connected spinal, back, or musculoskeletal injury.

topic.name: Fishery Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the conservation and management of fisheries in the United States, particularly through amendments to the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act.

Document 1: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
Requires fishery management plans to specify a time period for ending overfishing
Document 2: This bill amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
State jurisdiction over fisheries in the Gulf of Mexico is expanded to nine nautical miles
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Commerce from issuing any permit for commercial finfish aquaculture operations

topic.name: Federal Legislation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to federal employee protections, prosecution agreements, and preservation programs.

Document 1: whistle-blower protections for a Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) employee
prohibits an FBI or Department of Justice (DOJ) employee from taking or failing to take a personnel action
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to issue guidelines delineating when U.S. attorneys should enter into deferred prosecution agreements
Requires oversight of a deferred prosecution agreement by a federal monitor appointed by an independent third party
Document 3: amends the National Recording Preservation Act of 2000 to reauthorize through FY2026
the number of members of the Board of Directors of the National Recording Preservation Foundation shall be increased from 9 to 12

topic.name: Environmental Legislation and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at environmental protection, specifically concerning lead exposure and water recycling initiatives.

Document 1: amends the Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA) to exclude from the definition of 'abatement' any activity
the EPA must promulgate regulations to permit a resident owner of a dwelling that is target housing to authorize a contractor to forego compliance
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of Phase I permanent facilities
Limits the federal share of the cost of the project to 25%

topic.name: Government Legislation on Public Services
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving public services in mental health, energy security, and education.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to cover marriage and family therapist services and mental health counselor services under Medicare part B
Authorizes marriage and family therapists and mental health counselors to develop discharge plans for post-hospital services.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to submit to Congress a national intelligence assessment on national security and energy security issues
the short- and long-term outlook for prices, supply, and demand for key forms of energy such as crude oil, natural gas, and alternative fuels.
Document 3: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award renewable one-year STEP scholarships
Authorizes the Secretary to provide student loan forgiveness to borrowers under the Federal Family Education Loan and Direct Loan programs.

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Public Benefit
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving public welfare, including tax reforms and healthcare provisions.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the inclusion of any social security or tier I railroad retirement benefits in gross income.
Expresses the sense of Congress against using tax increases to provide revenue necessary to carry out this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a 20-year depreciation recovery period for heating, ventilation, air conditioning, or commercial refrigeration systems.
Calculated on a straight line basis for nonresidential buildings.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to use the Secretary's authority to employ certain personnel without regard to civil service or classification laws.
Conditions an uncompensated physician's receipt of a credential or privilege to practice medicine in a VA facility on the physician's commitment to serve at least 40 hours.

topic.name: Maritime Infrastructure and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses the regulatory frameworks and infrastructure development related to maritime activities, including natural gas facilities, port operations, and Arctic navigation.

Document 1: requires an applicant for approval under the Natural Gas Act to identify each employee or agent engaged in activities to persuade communities
maintain a publicly available database listing all such employees and agents.
Document 2: sets the tonnage requirement for consideration of dredging of small ports by the Corps of Engineers at a minimum of 500,000 tons.
Document 3: expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary of State should work to establish agreements to promote coordinated action among Arctic nations
requires a study on the feasibility and potential of establishing a deep water sea port in the Arctic.

topic.name: Public Policy Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at improving access to healthcare, education, and services for specific communities.

Document 1: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
Revises the list of entities to which sales by a manufacturer of covered outpatient drugs at nominal prices shall be considered to be sales at a nominal price.
Document 2: Authorizes the President to expand U.S.-Mexico academic exchange programs
Priority should be given to strengthening ties between communities and academic institutions.

topic.name: Military and National Guard Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the structure, authority, and operations of the National Guard and other military branches in the United States.

Document 1: Expands the authority of the Chief of the National Guard Bureau to include membership on the Joint Chiefs of Staff.
Requires an annual plan for the use of the Armed Forces and National Guard for responding to disasters and acts of terrorism.
Document 2: Modifies the Coast Guard Academy cadet nomination process by requiring that half of each incoming class be composed of cadets nominated by various members of Congress.
Document 3: Expands the eligibility of certain commissioned officers of the Armed Forces for the basic educational assistance program of the Department of Veterans Affairs.

topic.name: Property Rights and Economic Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at protecting property rights while promoting economic development and tourism through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Power Act regarding licenses for construction of dams to consider property rights.
Encourages private investment and recreational use by considering private landownership.
Document 2: Revises qualifications for the Corporation for Travel Promotion to enhance travel marketing efforts.
Includes U.S. territories in the Corporation's international travel promotion plan.
Document 3: Establishes a Nonprofit Capacity Building program to assist small and midsize nonprofit organizations.
Requires matching grants to support organizational development for nonprofits facing resource challenges.

topic.name: Legislation on Tax and Industry Standards
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative acts related to tax credits, fishing industry support, and electronic signature standards.

Document 1: Home Energy Affordability Tax Relief Act of 2014
income-based refundable tax credit for energy costs
Document 2: amends the Saltonstall-Kennedy Act
American Fisheries Advisory Committee
Document 3: Electronic Signature Standards Act of 2016
require the Internal Revenue Service to publish guidance for electronic signatures

topic.name: Land Management and Native Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal land management, Native American land rights, and the implications of federal actions on local communities and educational funding.

Document 1: convey specified property (22.98 acres) to the Yukon Kuskokwim Health Corporation
Shields the Corporation from liability for any environmental contamination on the conveyed property.
Document 2: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM)
transfers all interest of the United States in certain land which shall be held in trust for the Washoe Tribe of Nevada and California.

Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to make permanent amendments made to the Impact Aid program
require the valuation of the federal property located within the boundaries of an LEA by calculating the valuation.
